features.evidence: it s no surprise that most of the videos that you see on this channel are based around the use of this laptop and today i ll share my experience on using it over the last few years and even offer some advice for someone looking for one of these or even one of the newer models that literally dropped earlier this year so with that let s begin this is one of the first and only laptops i ve owned and one of the most premium ones too for that matter which i bought specifically for university this is the dell xps 15 9510 model to be precise with the rtx 3050 ti gpu 16 gb ram and a 512 gb ssd i bought it for around 1,500 brand new a couple years back which is around 1,800 as a baseline and around 30 discount from the original price due to some nice student discounts for reference now i know this laptop does have its set of issues as all laptops do and i m not going to be hiding those either in this video namely in the cooling department and the retail price but i think let s just start with a quick overview of the hardware so the boxing experience is pretty solid i will say it s not just a macbook clone in regards to the box and though a little bit bland it is a premium experience along with the standard stuff the charger and the paperwork i did appreciate the usbc to hdmi and usba adapter you get in the box which still works by the way moving onto the actual laptop itself aesthetics are also really well done with full aluminum external body a cnc mil as opposed to being stamped and has these diamond cut edges which if you catch just at the right light looks really good without overdoing it in regards to ports on the left you have two thunderbolt 3 ports and a kensington lock port which i ve never used once and on the right you have a usbc port which is not thunderbolt a full siize sd card slot and then a headphone jack overall definitely slim pickings regarding the ports with no usba or hdmi especially now that macbooks have partially reversed and added the hdmi back i generally don t connect many devices to my laptop or if i do it s at home where i have lots of adapters and i usually carry one in my bag as well again the adapter that was included in the box was pretty useful here right so now opening the laptop up which you can do with one finger by the way we have a few things here one of the main features being the 16 by10 infinity display yeah you get used to it over time but you really do appreciate it when you use someone else s laptop with a 16x9 display with large bezels plus it keeps the footprint of the device as small as it can be which is nice again i m not going to go into all the details regarding the panel given the laptop s age but if you are interested then here they are on screen right now moving over to the keyboard i think it s definitely worth talking about the carbon fiber keyboard deck new xps decks are made from glass in this department but i have to say that this carbon fiber deck is really a brilliant idea it ages well and wipes down easily too it s way better than those surface laptops with the alcanta where it s nice to begin with but then just stains and becomes disgusting over time trust me i ve seen some surface laptops where it s now permanently obvious that they had an accident involving their coffee and their laptop besides just the durability it s also a great feeling as a palm wrist and really appreciate it on those colder days after traveling when the metal exterior of the laptop is freezing and uncomfortable to touch while the palm rest is just a neutral temperature you get the benefits of an aluminium design without the coldness on your palms next we have the keyboard which again is certainly a very positive point for this device being extremely great for typing on and offers a good balance between those thinkpad keyboards you get and the updated macbook ones again this is something that you just have to try out in order to get an idea of this the layout as well is also pretty standard with no random keys or gimmicky buttons yes i am looking at you new xps 16 with the co pilot button and the capacitive function keys but we ll get on to that a bit later i would say that they could have had some functionality on the f9 key but i get they provide separation with the icons and the word keys as well including when the backlight is on which is quite good too i would say the arrow keys and the layout doesn t bother me either and just how it should be in my opinion rather than the smaller style for the left and right arrow keys moving on below we have the trackpad oh man i could probably speak about windows laptops and trackpads for a while but i think that s for another video the trackpad though not utilized by windows to its potential r macbook trackpads regarding a cheer size and certainly is very useful for general tasks dragging and dropping stuff and not accidentally hitting the edge all the time one tip i would give while on trackpads is turning off the right click in the right corner setting there s just no need for this if you need to right click don t memorize that you need to press this specific quadrant on the trackpad to activate it just tap with two fingers wherever you are on the trackpad it s so much quicker and easier this way another positive point for this device though i was tempted to put it into the negatives is surprisingly the speakers so over overall there are four speakers on the laptop two of each face upwards on the keyboard deck and two face downwards again something to note here is that the speakers don t occupy this whole space but rather just a small section at the bottom nevertheless together they do fill a room and sound pretty decent especially with the top firing speakers as you can put this on a soft surface for example and your sound is not completely gone again i haven t really used many other laptop speakers to compare to but i only use these speakers now rather than dedicated ones to keep my desk clear so make what you will of that also i m not an order feel far either the only thing that i would say which is the reason that i was going to put this into the negative section is that depending on the music or the frequency that is being played by the speakers this can cause a bit of a crackling or buzzy sound and no it s not the speakers distorting at max volume or anything but rather the keyboard and the metal components in some of the big keys vibrating so that s a backspace shift keys enter caps lock etc i find that it s most prominent on stuff piano music but other stuff voices and other sounds are just fine now again this is very situational depending on the frequency of sound that s being played the volume that you play at and if you happen to have your finger just covering that key but just thought to mention it as it seemed to be a lot of people speaking about this issue on reddit but no reviewers mentioned it now i don t really want to go into performance metrics that much as there s already plenty of videos that comprehensively go into these details instead i m going to focus on a real world use generally performance has been great for the laptop so my use case generally is office applications email word powerpoint and stuff that occasional casual gaming such as ste more epic games on medium to high settings i m fine with 60 fps to be honest cuz i don t have a 120 hz monitor unfortunately i do some 3d modeling occasionally so this is solid works and occasionally some simulation programs such as anis for example then more recently video production and video editing with d vinci resolve word and office applications are fine there s no issues here really as you d expect the laptop might get a little warm with many tabs opening chrome but generally you won t hear the fans for gaming yes the fans are loud and probably gets quite a bit hot as well but i use a mouse and keyboard for that along with some headphones so it s not too bothering in regards to 3d modeling if you do something similar solid works it should handle this just fine i think it depends on your program and the specific task that you re doing for me i was more on the modeling side of things and just being able to have a machine on the goal that i could just load a model up or something and do some tweaks to and the recent endeavor has been dci resolve and video editing this overall has been running well on the laptop i would probably not recommend doing 4k video editing as the timeline scrubbing is just not smooth when you play back or work in full resolution so i prefer to use proxies instead to lower it to half or quarter of the quality again i could have a setting or something that s not quite up to date but generally working in 1080p doesn t really affect me all too much i of interest here s the time that it took me to render out this video you re watching right now so make what you will of this something that i have learned though is keeping up to date with your drivers so for my use case it was the nvidia drivers and making sure that i downloaded the most recent one that nvidia is offering whether that be the studio driver or the game ready driver another thing that gravitated me towards this laptop was a repairability two ram slots and two ssd slots fully upgradeable in this form factor that was something i was definitely interested in especially as i generally keep my tech until it decomposes i haven t upgraded it yet but plan to very soon for storage and considering that there are two slots in here i can just pop another drive in without affecting the boot drive so yeah that should be simple enough hopefully as long as it don t explode anything obviously right so that takes me into the negative aspects that i have to say about this laptop first up honestly windows after using a laptop for more than 3 years following the use of a desktop for forever it s clear that windows is fundamentally more suited to your typical desktop pc rather than laptops sure it gets the job done and is 90 of what you need but i ve had quite a few driver problems over the years as well as optimizations with the trackpad and windows as a whole really why can i not swipe with two fingers to go back in file explorer for example windows needs to copy mc gestures regarding the hardware there s not really been many issues the computer does get a bit warm even on basic tasks cuz there could be some random system task going on in the background but i m not too sensitive to the heat i would say though that there was some light bleed on my laptop which i think is normal but again really shouldn t be a problem on laptops costing this much my trackpad thankfully though was not plagued with the wobbly issue which was still in the process of being fixed when this laptop was on sale other negatives i would say it s probably battery life honestly the battery life probably lasts around 4 to 5 hours on a single charge and more towards two to three if you do heavier tasks which is not the best i will say again i think this is some more to do with the cycle counts over the years as this has been my only desktop computing device for the last 3 years but still the webcam is also another negative point for this laptop and there s a reason why it s being bought up this late in the video and that s because it s a really bad 720p webcam it s just grainy noisy and looks only decent in a really well lit environment i get that the bezel is super small but with the newer models of xps they have a 1080p webcam in the same space so clearly this was a corner that was cut at the time by the way this is a quick voice recording using the buil in microphones on the xps 15 overall it s not the best but let me know what you think in the comments below also be sure to take the stickers off from your laptop when you get it as i ve kept mine on to show the intel badge of honor if you want to call it that but now in certain lighting that section has a slight color difference underneath the sticker so i m not sure whether to leave it on or take it off now that it s starting to peel away another negative aspect that i didn t think was going to make too much of a difference as it seems common practice on windows laptops nowadays is the fact that performance is limited if you re not connected to the charger generally in most tasks you won t notice any difference but if you use your gpu at all then you ll certainly notice this for example when playing games frame rates will be significantly lower without being plugged in and certain apps the vure isol scrubbing rendering or creating proxies just dramatically get smoother when you suddenly connect the charger so yeah i think this is just a general theme with windows laptops that you should probably be aware about because i didn t think it was going to make as big of a difference as it did not to mention battery life in all this as well so as a long term user considering the pros and cons what do i think of the new versus old xps design if you re in the market for this type of device so in my opinion i would consider the overall refresh of the xps line be of a net downgrade in many ways compared to an improvement for example there improvement in the performance sure a higher refresh display a better webcam slightly better battery maybe improved trackpad design but also many downsides for example working through these one at a time storage and ram upgrades are gone you can take the ssd out sure but that s about it compared to the older design where you get two sl for ram and storage for upgrading next are the ports generally they re the same but the sd card is now a micro sd card instead which is just not convenient especially for 14in and 16 in laptops when even the macbooks are adding several ports back no charging light yeah i know this is a trivial one but it s such a nice feature to not have to turn your laptop on to find out whether it s charged or not the left control key it seems this will be another system button in the sense that it now matches the position of the windows key but on the right now this means we have the co pilot key this is definitely a long term play by microsoft but not sure how well this is going to age compared to the traditional control key seen on 99 of windows keyboards and finally saving the biggest to last is the keyboard and the capacitive buttons now i will say that this aesthetic really does make it one of the best looking laptops on the market yes even beating the macbooks in my opinion but at what cost from early reviews this just looks a nightmare to use it literally offers no advantage over physical keys and considering the integration with windows laptops and the hardware i can almost guarantee that there will be times of unresponsiveness and driver issues with this as opposed to physical keys i get that they did this for a better cooling system but again based on current reviews i m not sure about how effective this is at least you have to give it to apple the touch bar offered some benefits in applications and had a certain amount of optimization compared to physical keys but even then they abandoned it as well so this speaks volumes here and i think now is a good time to address the elephant in the room why have i not upgraded to windows 11 well long story short i plan to very soon but considering that i relied on this laptop daily and also considering various software that was licensed i couldn t afford to deal with any driver or other technical issues and hence have been putting it off in addition even a good year and a half into the windows 11 life cycle there appeared to be concerns with the battery life on laptops and other smaller issues which again led me to stay on windows 10 for longer than i would have liked i feel these issues would have mostly been resolved after about 3 years now but yeah generally speaking windows 11 seems to be more of a facelift than any super critical functionality however now that i m at a point where i have some time in my hands and in the process of expanding my storage with a second ssd i plan to do this upgrade very soon as part of a rebuild and reinstallation process of windows so overall then yeah this device is not for everyone so overall then yeah this device is not for everyone and you re certainly paying a premium especially if you don t get any discounts off the retail price but personal personally i would say that for the right person this might be the only good windows laptop this is because it generally nails the fundamentals and one of the main categories where windows laptops continue to be flawed and don t really offer a lots of compelling options sure there are gaming laptops with better performance but at the cost of three times of thickness there are laptops with amazing high refresh screens but the body is made out of 100 recycled plastic there are also super repairable laptops but at the cost of a much larger retail premium you get the point this was the dilemma that i faced when selecting a windows laptop a couple years back and though offerings have improved there s not really anything that comes close in my opinion to the xps line in regards to an overall device from the overall premium feel of the device and the screen to the keyboard and trackpad for productivity based tasks with some casual gaming the xps line is certainly a solid option especially if you can get them for a good discount or even getting last year s model instead anyway that s been a long term review of this particular xps15 model if you found this video valuable then please share the video and if you really enjoyed then make sure to subscribe but thanks for watching and i ll see you later
